operator.note: Shifts the actor from agent-as-discoverer to platform/curator-as-snapper. Distinct from the whiteboard's verifier-patching and definitional-discovery items: those are about edge-case spec changes; this is about a post-processing operator that converts near-feasible into valid, a different mechanism entirely.

question: Treat integer-snapping as a SECOND, undeclared verifier that runs only on the winner: agents reach C(x)≈1e-15 (numerically near-feasible), then a post-hoc snapping step rounds coordinates to exact integers to manufacture a provably-valid construction. Audit what fraction of headline discoveries are 'snap-dependent' — invalid as submitted, valid only after the platform's rounding ritual — and ask whether the real discovery was the agent's search or the curator's choice of which near-misses deserve to be snapped into legitimacy. Who holds the snapping pen, and does snap-eligibility silently decide authorship?

floor.minimal_experiment: Data/setup: take the headline/leaderboard discoveries from a snapping-capable construction platform (e.g. the EinsteinArena lineage, arXiv:2606.10402v1, or any agent-discovery harness whose pipeline ends in coordinate rounding). For each discovery recover TWO artifacts: (i) the agent's SUBMITTED point x_raw at the moment search stopped (pre-snap continuous coords), and (ii) the published snapped point x_snap. Measure to test: re-run the platform's OWN declared verifier C(.) on x_raw at the platform's OWN declared tolerance/integrality rules, and classify each discovery as snap-INDEPENDENT (x_raw already valid) vs snap-DEPENDENT (x_raw invalid, only x_snap passes). Report the snap-dependent fraction f, and the snap displacement ||x_snap - x_raw|| distribution. Falsifiable prediction: the platform's implicit claim is that snapping is a cosmetic formatting step, which predicts f is near-zero (null: f<5%, and snaps are sub-tolerance nudges). Reject if f is materially large (e.g. >25% of headline results are invalid-as-submitted), OR if snap displacement routinely exceeds the verifier tolerance band — i.e. snapping is doing load-bearing feasibility repair, not formatting. Control group: (1) re-run the same x_raw->snap on a set of agent runs that the curator did NOT select/publish (rejected near-misses) to show snap-eligibility is a curatorial choice, not an automatic property — if rejected near-misses snap to valid at the same rate as published ones, the curator's selection (not the search) is what conferred legitimacy; (2) a synthetic control of genuinely-from-scratch integer constructions (no rounding step) to confirm f≈0 is achievable, ruling out that snapping is universally unavoidable.
floor.falsifiable_prediction: If snapping were merely cosmetic formatting (the platform's implicit claim), the fraction of headline discoveries that are invalid-as-submitted but valid-only-after-snap will be near zero (<5%) and snap displacements will sit below the verifier's tolerance band. The reframe predicts the opposite for at least one real platform: a materially large snap-dependent fraction (>25%) and/or snap displacements that exceed tolerance, meaning the rounding step is performing load-bearing feasibility repair. Stronger sub-prediction: published near-misses and curator-rejected near-misses snap-to-valid at indistinguishable rates, exposing snap-eligibility as a curatorial selection rather than a property the agent earned.
floor.control: Two controls. (1) Curator-rejected near-misses run through the identical snap operator: if they reach validity at the same rate as published winners, legitimacy came from the curator's selection, not the agent's search (isolates the authorship claim). (2) Genuinely from-scratch integer constructions with NO rounding step, to confirm snap-dependent fraction f≈0 is attainable and snapping is not a universal physical necessity of the problem class (rules out the trivial-everything-needs-rounding explanation).
floor.reject_reason:
[discriminating] Refuted on TWO independent grounds — the prediction is non-trivially-but-already-conceded (strawman null), AND the load-bearing artifact + control groups are unobtainable from the named platform.

(1) THE NULL IS A STRAWMAN THE PLATFORM NEVER ASSERTS. The experiment frames the platform's "implicit claim" as: snapping is cosmetic formatting, predicting f<5% and sub-tolerance nudges. But arXiv:2606.10402v1 explicitly states the OPPOSITE in plain text. On the headline kissing-number result it writes: inner products were "close to simple integer values... though not exactly equal... so the agent applied a final integer-snapping post-processing step to make these values exact, resulting in a provably valid construction... the agents first found a numerically near-valid configuration and then converted it into an exact discrete structure that the verifier could certify." And: "the integer-snapping technique, more critically [than the surrogate loss], allowed us to push the construction to n=600." So snap-as-load-bearing-feasibility-repair is the authors' own account, not a hidden finding. The "falsifiable prediction" is therefore non-discriminating: the reframe's predicted outcome (snap is load-bearing) is already documented, so re-deriving it cannot distinguish the reframe ("the curator holds the snapping pen / authorship is curatorial") from the platform's own stated view ("snapping is a legitimate construction technique the agent applied"). Confirming f is large tells you nothing about WHO conferred legitimacy — the load-bearing fact is compatible with both hypotheses. The genuinely contested claim (snap-eligibility = curatorial choice, not earned property) is precisely the sub-prediction that needs the control group, which fails on ground (2).

(2) THE REQUIRED ARTIFACTS DO NOT EXIST IN THE PLATFORM'S DATA. The experiment's whole measurement hinges on recovering x_raw = "the agent's SUBMITTED point at the moment search stopped (pre-snap continuous coords)" for each discovery, plus two control sets. The paper's retention policy makes all three unobtainable: "rejected or lower-scoring submissions are not added to the leaderboard and not stored in our database... only ... all personal-best submissions are retained." (a) x_raw with C(x_raw)≈1e-15 is by construction a LOWER-scoring submission than its snapped twin (C=0) for the same agent on the same problem — i.e. exactly the non-personal-best class that is discarded. The DB keeps the certified (post-snap) construction, not the continuous pre-snap point; for many discoveries x_raw was never even submitted (snapping is a local post-process the agent ran before submitting). (b) Control group 1 (curator-rejected near-misses) is explicitly not stored — "rejected... submissions are not stored in our database" — so the central discriminating test (do rejected and published near-misses snap-to-valid at indistinguishable rates?) cannot be run on real data at all. Without it, the authorship/curation claim is untestable on this platform. The auditor cannot conjure x_raw or the rejected set; they are gone.

SECONDARY: the verifier C(.) IS the continuous proxy, and a valid construction is defined as C(x*)=0 exactly (binary feasibility), so "re-run C(.) on x_raw at the platform's tolerance" is ill-posed — there is no published tolerance band δ for feasibility (δ is the leaderboard improvement threshold, a different object); any x_raw with C>0 is simply invalid, making f trivially = (fraction that needed any snap) and turning the >25% test into a near-tautology rather than a discriminating measurement.

The synthetic control (ground-up integer constructions giving f≈0) is executable and fine, but it only rules out "snapping is universally unavoidable" — a claim no one makes — and cannot rescue (1) or (2). The proposal is a genuine, sharp critique, but as an EXPERIMENT it is non-discriminating (predicts an already-admitted fact) and practically inexecutable on the named platform (the pre-snap point and the rejected-near-miss control are, by the platform's own stated policy, not retained). refuted=true. ｜ [so_what] No named actor flips a concrete decision on the audit's output. The headline-validity decision is immune: a snapped construction is independently exact-verifiable (plug integers into C, get exactly 0), so whether 5% or 95% of results are "snap-dependent" changes no reviewer's, funder's, or buyer's accept/believe choice — each result is validated post-snap on its own. The provocative "who holds the snapping pen / does snap-eligibility decide authorship" framing rests on an unestablished premise: that snapping is human-discretionary curation rather than a deterministic rounding rule. If it's deterministic (the stated C≈1e-15→round flow), no curator "chooses which near-misses deserve legitimacy" and the authorship question dissolves; if it were discretionary, the question never shows that, so the audit can't establish it either. The only decision that could flip (a journal/benchmark changing credit policy) requires first proving discretionary gating — which is a different, unasked investigation. Against frameshift's own record this is also a restatement of the already-logged core insight (验证器≠真值 / "那12个数学问题算不算新发现"), recast as a fraction to compute. Computing the snap-dependent fraction deepens understanding of where the labor sits but names no person who, on seeing the number, does something different. Classic so-what: interesting framing, zero decision lever.

value_profile.valuable_to_whom: Operators/auditors of agentic scientific-discovery and constructive-math leaderboards (the curators themselves), peer reviewers and competition judges who must decide what counts as a valid submitted construction, and benchmark-integrity researchers studying whether 'AI discovered X' claims survive when the post-processing pipeline is disclosed. Directly actionable for the frameshift platform's own appraisal station, which faces the same snap-vs-search authorship question.

prior_art.known_name: Specification gaming / Goodhart via a post-hoc projection-repair operator — the snap is rounding/projection of a relaxed (near-feasible continuous) solution onto the integer feasible set, the same move as LP-relaxation rounding and projection-onto-feasible-set in integer optimization; the 'who gets snapped' angle is curator-as-coauthor / construct-validity gaming.
prior_art.novelty_watermark: Known mechanism (rounding-a-relaxation + Goodhart/spec-gaming) operationalized in a genuinely new scene: reframing the rounding step as an UNDECLARED SECOND VERIFIER that runs only on the curator-selected winner, and converting that into a concrete authorship/attribution audit (snap-dependent fraction + rejected-near-miss control). The measurement protocol and the authorship-shift question are the fresh contribution, not the underlying mechanism.

lenses.reason:
这条提交在机制上与已收方向均不同,可单独设计实验区分,不构成换皮重复。逐条排查:

[3] (dof2) 最接近,因为同样援引 n=604 vs integer-604 这一情节。但 [3] 攻击的假设是「discovery 的计分单元应是 generalization 的 reach 而非单实例的 magnitude」,其机制是把验证对象从「单点答案」改为「参数族/猜想的可机检泛化」,实验是重新计分看排序是否重排。本条攻击的假设完全不同:它把 integer-snapping 当作一个「未声明的第二验证器」,只对 winner 运行,质疑的是「near-feasible (C≈1e-15) → 取整 → 可证有效」这一后处理流水线本身的合法性,以及「谁有权决定哪些 near-miss 值得被取整封圣 (snap-eligibility)」这一策展/作者权问题。[3] 完全不涉及 snapping 这道工序、不涉及「winner-only 二次验证器」、不涉及 curator 的取整决策权。两者只是共享同一个 motivating example,但解剖的机制和可检验的对象正交。

[1] (dof1) 攻击 KILL 决策的价值,机制是 anti-portfolio 日志;本条攻击的是 ACCEPT 后的取整工序,无关。

[2]/[4] (dof6) 攻击 operator 的房间优势/问责层缺失;本条的 curator 权力不是关于信息不对称或身份问责,而是关于「snap pen 持有者对 near-miss 的封圣裁量」这一具体技术工序的作者权归属,机制不同。

[5] (dof4) 攻击 discussion board 经 Llama-Guard 的真值盲传播;本条不涉及 board 传播,涉及的是 verifier pipeline 内部一道隐藏验证步骤。

[6] (dof4) 攻击「优化代理的代理 (surrogate-of-proxy)」,即 agent 私有目标偏离 C(x);本条恰好相反方向——它关注的是 agent 已经把 C(x) 推到 ≈1e-15 之后、平台侧再施加的取整动作,即 verifier 端而非 agent 端的失配。[6] 在 solution-space 之前,本条在 verifier 判定之后。

可单独检验的独立实验:对全部 headline discoveries 做审计,测量「snap-dependent」比例(提交态 C(x) 非零、仅取整后才精确可行)、刻画 snap-eligibility 判定规则、并检验「真发现在 agent 的搜索还是 curator 的取整裁量」——这套审计与上述任何一条的实验设计都不重合。机制独立,refuted=false。
